stem: How many integers \( n \) with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) are divisible by [[a]] or by [[b]] (or by both)?
solution: By inclusion-exclusion (and since \( [[a]] \) and \( [[b]] \) are coprime), the count is \( \lfloor [[N]]/[[a]] \rfloor + \lfloor [[N]]/[[b]] \rfloor - \lfloor [[N]]/([[a]]\cdot[[b]]) \rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the numbers counted twice (divisible by both).
This adds the overlap instead of subtracting it.
This counts only the integers divisible by both [[a]] and [[b]].
This counts only the multiples of [[a]].
This counts only the multiples of [[b]].
This is off from the correct count by one.

stem: How many integers \( n \) with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) are divisible by at least one of [[a]], [[b]], or [[c]]?
solution: By inclusion-exclusion on the three (pairwise coprime) divisors: \( \left(\lfloor N/a\rfloor+\lfloor N/b\rfloor+\lfloor N/c\rfloor\right) - \left(\lfloor N/ab\rfloor+\lfloor N/ac\rfloor+\lfloor N/bc\rfloor\right) + \lfloor N/abc\rfloor = [[answer]] \), with \(N=[[N]]\), \(a=[[a]]\), \(b=[[b]]\), \(c=[[c]]\).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract any of the pairwise overlaps.
This subtracts all three pairwise overlaps but forgets to add back the triple overlap.
This counts only the integers divisible by all three simultaneously.
This subtracts instead of adds the individual counts, an unrelated formula.
This is off from the correct count by one.
This is off from the correct count by one.

stem: How many integers \( n \) with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) are divisible by EXACTLY ONE of [[a]] or [[b]] (that is, by one of them but not both)?
solution: Start from the "at least one" count \( \lfloor N/a\rfloor+\lfloor N/b\rfloor-\lfloor N/ab\rfloor \), then remove the numbers divisible by BOTH (counted once there but belonging to neither exclusive group) once more: \( \lfloor [[N]]/[[a]]\rfloor+\lfloor [[N]]/[[b]]\rfloor-2\lfloor [[N]]/([[a]][[b]])\rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the "at least one" (divisible by [[a]] or [[b]]) count, which still includes numbers divisible by both.
This forgets to remove the overlap at all.
This counts only the integers divisible by both [[a]] and [[b]], the opposite of what was asked.
This subtracts one count from the other instead of using the symmetric-difference formula.
This is off from the correct count by one.
This is off from the correct count by one.

stem: How many integers \( n \) with \( 1 \le n \le [[N]] \) are divisible by NEITHER [[a]] NOR [[b]]?
solution: By De Morgan and inclusion-exclusion, the count divisible by [[a]] or [[b]] is \( \lfloor N/a\rfloor+\lfloor N/b\rfloor-\lfloor N/ab\rfloor \); subtracting this from the total \(N=[[N]]\) leaves those divisible by neither: \( [[N]] - \lfloor [[N]]/[[a]]\rfloor - \lfloor [[N]]/[[b]]\rfloor + \lfloor [[N]]/([[a]][[b]])\rfloor = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the count divisible by [[a]] or [[b]], the complement of what was asked.
This subtracts both individual counts from the total but forgets to add back the double-subtracted overlap.
This only removes the numbers divisible by both, leaving in those divisible by exactly one.
This reports the raw "or" count before ever subtracting from the total.
This is off from the correct count by one.
This is off from the correct count by one.

stem: A committee of [[k]] people is to be chosen from a group of [[n]] people that includes two particular people, X and Y. In how many ways can the committee be selected so that AT LEAST ONE of X or Y is included?
solution: By the complement rule, subtract the committees using NEITHER X nor Y (chosen from the other \( [[n]]-2 \) people) from the total: \( \binom{[[n]]}{[[k]]} - \binom{[[n]]-2}{[[k]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to exclude any committees and counts all of them.
This is the count of committees with NEITHER X nor Y, the complement of what was asked.
This double-counts committees containing both X and Y, since it never subtracts that overlap.
This only accounts for including X, ignoring the possibility of including Y instead.
This is off from the correct count by one.
This is off from the correct count by one.
